meta.title: RAG & Content Generation Prompt Library
meta.purpose: Structured prompts for drafting glossaries, JSONL chunks, ingest scaffolding, grounding wrappers, and review passes for the financial literacy chatbot.
meta.disclaimer: Generated drafts require human fact-checking against course materials and primary sources. Do not treat model output as legal, tax, or personalized financial advice.

always_include: Educational framing only; no personalized financial, tax, or legal advice.
Never recommend specific banks, brokerages, funds, stocks, or credit cards by name.
Use hypothetical or illustrative numbers only unless citing a user-provided value.
Plain English suitable for early-career adults (approx. grades 10–14 reading level).
output_hygiene: Ask for Markdown or JSONL only when specified; no surrounding commentary unless requested.
Label uncertain factual claims with [verify] for manual review.

prompts.title: Glossary & topic markdown (data/rag/*.md)
prompts.model_role: You are helping build an educational (not advisory) financial literacy glossary for college students and early-career professionals.
prompts.user_template:
Constraints:
- No specific product, bank, fund, or stock names.
- No personalized advice; use "many people" / "in general" framing.
- Short definitions (2–5 sentences), plain English.
- Include a "Common misconceptions" bullet where useful.

Output format: Markdown with ## headings per term.

Topics to cover (one section each): {{topics}}

For each term, include:
- Definition
- Why it matters
- One hypothetical example (clearly labeled as illustrative)

If a detail varies by employer, loan type, or jurisdiction, say so briefly and add [verify] instead of guessing.

prompts.title: RAG chunks as JSONL (data/rag_index/chunks.jsonl)
prompts.model_role: You produce machine-readable retrieval chunks for a RAG pipeline used in an educational financial literacy chatbot.
prompts.user_template:
I need a JSONL file. Each line must be exactly one JSON object with keys: id, text, source, topic

Rules:
- text: 150–400 words, self-contained, no markdown code fences inside text
- source: synthetic filename like "glossary.md" or "topic_credit.md"
- topic: one of [financial_foundations, budget_cashflow, credit_management, workplace_401k, student_loans, borrowing_basics]
- id: stable slug, e.g. "credit_utilization_001"

Generate {{chunk_count}} chunks total, distributed across topics as evenly as possible.
Educational only; no product recommendations; no account numbers or PII.

Output ONLY JSONL lines (one object per line). No commentary, no markdown fences.

prompts.title: Minimal ingest / retrieval Python (keyword or stub embeddings)
prompts.model_role: You write small, readable Python for a class project prototype.
prompts.user_template:
Write a minimal Python module that:
- Reads all .md files from a folder path argument
- Splits on ## headings into chunks
- Writes chunks.jsonl with objects: {id, text, source, heading}
- Implements retrieve(query: str, chunks: list[dict], k: int = 5) using simple keyword overlap scoring (tokenize on non-letters, lowercase); no third-party dependencies
- Includes a stub embed_text(s: str) -> list[float] that raises NotImplementedError with a comment on where to plug in an embedding API

Keep under ~180 lines, type hints optional, include if __name__ == "__main__": demo.

Project context: financial literacy RAG; chunks are educational text only.

prompts.title: System / developer snippet: use retrieved excerpts only
prompts.model_role: You write a reusable instruction block for an LLM that receives retrieved context.
prompts.user_template:
Write a single string suitable for a system or developer message in an OpenAI-style chat API. It must instruct the model to:
- Ground explanations primarily in the provided "Retrieved excerpts" section
- If excerpts do not support a claim, say the knowledge base does not specify it
- Never recommend specific financial products; explain decision criteria instead
- Maintain a supportive, non-judgmental educational tone
- End with a one-line disclaimer that this is educational information, not personalized advice

Do not include placeholders like {{excerpts}} inside the string; instead say "the excerpts provided in the user message" or similar.
Maximum ~120 words.

prompts.title: Review pass on an existing chunk
prompts.model_role: You are an editor for clarity and accessibility, not a source of new financial facts.
prompts.user_template:
Here is a glossary or RAG chunk for early-career adults. Improve clarity and reading flow. Do not add new factual claims. If a sentence may be wrong or oversimplified, rewrite cautiously or mark with [verify].

Constraints:
- No product names
- No personalized advice
- Keep length within ±15% of the original

---
{{chunk_text}}
---

prompts.title: Phase 4 plan generation with injected RAG context (orchestrator integration)
prompts.model_role: You are the Speaker for Phase 4 Plan Generation in a phase-based financial literacy chatbot.
prompts.user_template:
User profile and goal are in JSON below. Retrieved excerpts from our knowledge base are provided; they may be incomplete.

Produce a structured educational plan with these sections in order:
1) Situation summary (use user state; no internal field names)
2) Key concepts (ground in excerpts when possible; flag gaps)
3) Step-by-step checklist (actionable, non-product)
4) Risks and pitfalls (general patterns, not predictions)
5) 30-day action plan (weekly milestones)

If excerpts conflict, prefer cautious language and [verify] rather than asserting one version.

State JSON:
{{state_json}}

Retrieved excerpts:
{{retrieved_excerpts}}
